data.title: 15 addresses withdrew a total of 406,100 ETH in two days, worth $1.6 billion
data.description: According to @lookonchain monitoring, a total of 15 addresses withdrew 406,117 ETH from Kraken, Galaxy Digital, BitGo and FalconX in the past two days, worth approximately US$1.6 billion.

data.title: Google-Backed Crypto Miner TeraWulf Plans $3B Debt-Financed Expansion: Report
data.description:
Crypto miner TeraWulf plans to raise $3B through bonds or loans to expand data centers in a deal backed by Google.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/crypto-miner-terawulf-plans-3b-debt-financed-expansion/

data.title: Ethereum Developers Plan Fusaka Upgrade Activation on October 1
data.description:
According to PANews, the 221st Ethereum All Core Developers Execution (ACDE) meeting focused on the progress of the Fusaka upgrade public testnet. Although four client teams have yet to release their testnet versions, developers have unanimously agreed to activate the upgrade as scheduled on October 1.

The meeting also resolved to increase the default gas limit per block to 60 million following the Fusaka mainnet upgrade. Additionally, progress on testing related to the Glamsterdam upgrade's Ethereum Improvement Proposals (EIPs) was discussed. The EIP process will be optimized by assigning a contact person for each proposal.

An engineer from OpenZeppelin introduced EIP 7819, which aims to simplify the creation and updating of smart contract delegate accounts.

data.title: Aster: Compensation for XPL contract anomalies has now been fully paid out
data.description: Aster announced on the X platform that compensation for the XPL contract anomaly has been fully distributed. All affected users have received compensation directly in USDT. For any further questions, please submit a ticket through the official Discord channel.

data.title: WLFI to begin repo program this week
data.description: World Liberty Financial (WLFI) announced in a post on X that it will begin implementing a buyback program this week using liquidity fee revenue. All buybacks and token burns will be publicly and transparently announced after implementation. Previously, WLFI had approved a proposal to use all fees generated by the protocol's own liquidity to repurchase WLFI tokens in the market and permanently burn them.

data.title: A whale deposited 6.27 million USDC into HyperLiquid and bought 5.279 million XPL at an average price of $1.89.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale transferred 6.27 million USDC to HyperLiquid, and then bought 5.279 million XPL at an average price of US$1.89.

data.title: Stablecoin protocol Level announced that it has been acquired by a leading DeFi company and will gradually cease operations.
data.description: Level, a yield-generating stablecoin protocol, announced that it has been acquired by a leading DeFi firm, and the Level team will join the protocol in the future. As part of this transition, the Level protocol (including lvlUSD and slvlUSD) will be phased out. All users will be able to unstake slvlUSD and redeem lvlUSD, and all cooldown periods will be shortened to 2 seconds. The last batch of yields will be distributed on October 2, 2025, after which no yield will be generated, regardless of whether the asset remains staked. The front-end interface will remain operational until December 15, 2025, after which users will still be able to unstake and redeem directly through the smart contract.

data.title: The ETH band whale bought 7,632 ETH at an average price of $3,928.66, with some orders placed at $3,955 for profit.
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ai Yi @ai_9684 xtpa, address 0x54d…e6029 spent approximately $29.985 million five hours ago to purchase 7,632 ETH at an average price of $3,928.66. Subsequently, the address placed a limit sell order on Cowswap for 2,547 ETH at $3,955, aiming to partially take profits after a slight price increase. The corresponding position is estimated to be worth approximately $10 million.

data.title: Whales/institutions bought 60,000 ETH in batches over the past week, resulting in a $20 million loss
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ember (@EmberCN), a major whale/institutional investor has been engaged in a recent period of swing trading, purchasing a total of 60,333 ETH between September 20th and 26th, spending $257 million at an average price of $4,256. This represents a floating loss of approximately $20 million based on current prices. This includes: 16,569 ETH (US$74.3 million) purchased at $4,484 on September 20th; 20,000 ETH (US$85.95 million) purchased at $4,298 on September 22nd; 13,764 ETH (US$56.89 million) purchased at $4,133 on September 25th; and 10,000 ETH (US$39.65 million) purchased at $3,965 on September 26th. Previously, the address bought 132,000 ETH at an average price of $2,540 in June, and sold them in batches at an average price of around $2,923, with a cumulative profit of approximately $50 million.

data.title: Social trading app Share raises $5 million in funding, with participation from Coinbase Ventures and others
data.description: Share, a social trading app, announced it has raised $5 million in funding from investors including Coinbase Ventures, Collaborative+Currency, and Palm Tree Crypto. Launched by Scott Gray, founder of Genie, the app supports the three major public blockchains: Solana, Base, and Ethereum, offering on-chain transaction dynamics, wallet tracking, market information, and token trading. Each wallet automatically generates a Share profile, and users can link multiple wallets and Farcaster accounts. (Blockworks)

data.title: Aster: XPL contract anomaly due to internal configuration error, automatically compensate affected users
data.description: The Aster team announced that compensation for the XPL perpetual swap price anomaly will be automatically distributed to affected users within one hour. Any questions can be submitted via Discord. The official explanation stated that the incident stemmed from a parameter configuration error during the transition of XPL from the pre-launch market to the official perpetual market. This resulted in the index price being capped at $1 starting at 5:30 PM (UTC) on September 24th. Even after the cap was removed, the index remained in pre-launch mode, leading to an abnormal price spike between 9:36 PM and 9:45 PM. The issue was fully resolved by 9:45 PM and was confirmed to be an internal configuration error, not an external attack.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks fell across the board at the close of US stocks, with ALTS down 10.53%.
data.description: According to MyStonks data, at the close of US stocks, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.38% to 45,947 points; the S&P 500 fell 0.5% to 6,604 points; and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.5% to 22,384 points. ALTS fell approximately 10.53% to $3.06; MARA fell approximately 8.9% to $16.07; BLSH fell approximately 8.52% to $61.83; SBET fell approximately 7.22% to $16.31; MSTR fell approximately 6.99% to $300.7; and BTCS rose approximately 6.8% to $4.52. It is reported that MyStonks is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has launched hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stocks and ETF token targets such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

Its current holdings are valued at approximately $102 million, with a floating loss of approximately $13.18 million. data.title: DoraHacks Releases BUIDL AI 4.0: Automating the Full Lifecycle of Developer Relationships
data.description: DoraHacks, the global hackathon and developer ecosystem platform, has officially launched BUIDL AI 4.0: The DevRel AI. This new version focuses on developer relationship automation (DevRel Automation), helping organizations fully automate and efficiently manage BUIDL and developer communities. BUIDL AI 4.0 transforms the short-term hype of hackathons into long-term ecosystem value, bringing a brand new ecosystem-building experience to new technology platforms and developers.

data.title: U.S. Tariffs on Furniture and Pharmaceuticals to Increase in October
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. President Donald Trump has announced new tariffs set to take effect from October 1, 2025. A 50% tariff will be imposed on all kitchen cabinets, bathroom vanities, and related products. Additionally, upholstered furniture will face a 30% tariff. Heavy-duty trucks manufactured outside the United States will incur a 25% tariff. Furthermore, a 100% tariff will be applied to pharmaceutical products unless the company is establishing its manufacturing facilities within the U.S. These measures are part of the administration's efforts to encourage domestic production and reduce reliance on foreign imports.

data.title: Aave's V4 Update to Enhance DeFi Lending with Modular Markets
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, decentralized finance platform Aave is set to release its V4 update in the fourth quarter of 2025. This major protocol upgrade aims to introduce modular lending markets and enhanced risk controls, marking a significant evolution in the platform's capabilities.

The V4 update will implement a 'hub and spoke' modular design, allowing for more customized crypto borrowing and lending markets without confining liquidity to separate silos. Liquidity hubs will serve as central pools, while each spoke will represent a distinct market with one of three risk profiles, offering varied borrowing and lending rates. This replaces Aave's current uniform rate system. The update details that each spoke will register with the hub, draw liquidity, and upon repayment, return a base rate set at the hub level along with an asset-specific risk premium linked to its collateral composition.

Aave V4 will also feature a new user interface providing a unified, wallet-level view of all modular spokes. This will enable users to access detailed information and route trades through different market modules from a single overview. Additionally, the update will introduce dynamic risk configurations to prevent unexpected liquidations due to changes such as lowered collateral thresholds. Unlike Aave V3, where changing global parameters could risk liquidation if multiple positions were open, the new liquidation engine will adopt a 'health-targeted' model. This approach ensures that liquidations only cover enough to restore a loan to the desired collateral parameters, allowing lenders to collect while keeping the borrower's position open.

Users will have the option to utilize a 'Position Manager' that can automatically execute actions like withdrawal, borrowing, repayment, and other transaction management features. The update will also introduce a multi-call feature, enabling users to batch actions into a single transaction for more straightforward execution. Aave's V4 upgrade is anticipated to launch in the final quarter of 2025, with plans to release a whitepaper, make the V4 codebase public, and launch a testnet for the new version of the DeFi protocol.

The launch is eagerly awaited as the total value locked (TVL) in decentralized finance has surpassed $156 billion, nearing the peak levels seen in December 2021 during the previous bull market. This development underscores the growing interest and investment in DeFi platforms like Aave, which recently saw its total value locked surpass $40 billion in August.
